beats.narration_text: Halo. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of chsh bell inequality tester. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of chsh bell inequality tester

beats.narration_text: CHSH Bell Inequality Tester is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. E(a,b) = −cos(a−b) for the singlet. Drag Alice and Bob's angles — find the configuration that pushes |S| above 2 and toward 2√2 ≈ 2.828.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: CHSH Bell Inequality Tester

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: CHSH Bell Inequality Tester

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of CHSH Bell Inequality Tester. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: a₁, a₂, b₁. Verify: E(a,b) = −cos(a−b) for the singlet. Drag Alice and Bob's angles — find the configuration that pushes |S| above 2 and tow. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of CHSH Bell Inequality Tester. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: a₁, a₂, b₁. Verify: E(a,b) = −cos(a−b) for

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // CHSH Bell Inequality Tester — core physics

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: chsh bell inequality tester. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag a₁ from 0 to 360. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of a₁ from 0 to 360

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of a₁ from 0 to 360

beats.narration_text: E(a,b) = −cos(a−b) for the singlet. Drag Alice and Bob's angles — find the configuration that pushes |S| above 2 and toward 2√2 ≈ 2.828.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of chsh bell inequality tester. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this CHSH Bell Inequality Tester simulation and add a second slider that controls a₁. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this CHSH Bell Inequality Tester simulation and add a second slider that controls a₁. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
